title: scirpus pygmaeus
intro: Scirpus pygmaeus, also known as the dwarf bulrush or pygmy bulrush, is a small aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family. Traditionally, Scirpus pygmaeus has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, Scirpus pygmaeus is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be consulted with a medical professional before consumption.
